title: hieracium pseudalpinum subsp. cochlearioides
intro: Hieracium pseudalpinum subsp. cochlearioides, also known as hawkweed, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of Hieracium pseudalpinum subsp. cochlearioides include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or tea, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
